data.title: Warner Bros. bidding war: JPMorgan, Allen & Co set to earn $180M in M&A fees
data.description:
JPMorgan (JPM) and Allen & Company stand to earn $90M each for serving as advisers to Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), regardless of which company wins the bidding war for the media giant, according to an exchange filing.

Warner Bros. (WBD) paid JPMorgan (JPM) about $189M in fees two years prior to the deal for financial advisory and other services.

This included JPMorgan's (JPM) work on a bridge loan that allowed Warner Bros. (WBD) to pursue its separation into two companies.

JPMorgan (JPM) worked with Warner Bros. (WBD) for over two years on possible M&A options and came up with the plan to split the company in two, a source told Reuters.

For this, Warner Bros. (WBD) bought back about half of its bonds at a discount, in a deal that was financed with a $17.5B bridge loan from JPMorgan (JPM). It's said to be the largest non-investment-grade bridge loan ever made on Wall Street.

Netflix (NFLX) sweetened its offer for Warner Bros.' (WBD) streaming and studio assets this week. Investors now await updates from Paramount Skydance (PSKY), whose tender offer for the entire company closes on Wednesday.

data.title: Denmark's Frederiksen says sovereignty is non-negotiable after Trump's threat - AP
Karman: Seemann, MSC deal to boost access to U.S. Navy programs, lift 2026 results
data.description:
Karman Holdings’ (KRMN) shares climbed ~9% in premarket trading on Thursday after the company lifted its fiscal 2025 guidance, forecasting revenue of $470 million to $471 million and adjusted EBITDA of $144.5 million to $144.9 million.

For fiscal year 2026, it expects revenue between $700 million and $715 million and adjusted EBITDA between $205 million and $215 million

Analysts estimate revenue of $464.01M for the year ending Dec 2025 and $577.65M for the following year. 

Furthermore, Karman Space & Defense said the Seemann and MSC transaction, valued at $220 million, is expected to expand its access to multi-decade, high-priority, funded U.S. Navy programs and to be immediately accretive in 2026 to revenue growth, funded backlog, EBITDA, earnings per share, and cash flow.

“Our core business continues to thrive as we build greater scale and capabilities through strategic acquisitions,” said Tony Koblinski, Karman (KRMN) chief executive officer.

“From deep sea to deep space, Seemann and MSC will make us truly an all-domain solutions provider with a larger addressable market and greater funding visibility and revenue across more of the nation’s highest priority programs.”

Karman further anticipates that the acquisition will support its position at the upper echelon of adjusted EBITDA margins among defense technology companies.

Source: Filing

data.title: Wall Street set to extend rally as Greenland fears ease
data.description:
Stock index futures pointed to a higher open Thursday, adding to gains sparked by President Trump reversing course on new tariffs on European countries.

Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) advanced +0.96%. S&P 500 futures (SPX) rose +0.72% and Dow futures (INDU) gained +0.46%.

U.S. Treasury yields showed mixed movement, with the longer end slipping slightly. The 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) fell 0.6 basis points to 4.86% and the 10-year yield (US10Y) edged down 0.1 basis points to 4.24%. The shorter-end 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) ticked higher by 0.2 basis points to 3.60%.

"Whilst the Greenland news was the main driver boosting markets, another supportive factor was the start of the Supreme Court case into Lisa Cook’s removal from the Fed’s Board of Governors," Deutsche Bank's  Jim Reid said. "We don’t have a verdict yet, but the start of arguments showed that some of the conservative justices were questioning some of the Trump administration’s arguments."

Top gainers in premarket trading included Moderna (MRNA) +5.14%, Ares Management Corporation (ARES) +3.76%, and McKesson Corporation (MCK) +3.59%. Decliners included Otis Worldwide Corporation (OTIS) -3.43%, Labcorp Holdings Inc. (LH) -1.93%, and Ameriprise Financial (AMP) -1.47%.

data.title: Adtran sees Q4 revenue above forecast at $290M–$293M
data.description:
• Adtran (ADTN) Wedneseday said that Q4 preliminary revenue is expected to be in the range of $290.0 million to $293.0 million, exceeding the networking company’s previously announced guidance range of $275.0 million to $285.0 million. 
  • Full-year preliminary U.S. GAAP revenue is expected to be between $1,082.2 million and $1,085.2 million.
  • The preliminary non-GAAP operating margin for the fourth quarter is expected to be between 6.0% and 6.9%, which is within our previously announced guidance range of 3.5% to 7.5%.
  • Adtran (ADTN) rose 1.9% premarket.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Thursday: DDOG, BABA, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged higher in the premarket hours of Thursday as easing geopolitical fears fueled a broad market rally, with traders also eyeing a key morning inflation reading.

Biggest stock gainers

  • Karman Holdings (KRMN) +7% - Shares surged after the company raised its FY2025 outlook and issued a strong FY2026 forecast, driven by the Seemann and MSC acquisitions that expand its reach into high-priority U.S. Navy programs. Karman now expects FY2025 revenue of $470M–$471M, ahead of the consensus of $464.01M, with adjusted EBITDA of $144.5M–$144.9M, while FY2026 guidance also came in well above expectations with revenue of $700M–$715M (consensus $577.65M) and adjusted EBITDA of $205M–$215M. Management highlighted that the acquisitions will be immediately accretive across revenue, EBITDA, EPS, and cash flow, reinforcing the company’s position among high-margin defense technology peers.
  • Alibaba (BABA) +5% - Shares gained following a report that the company is preparing its chip unit, T-Head, for a potential IPO as it looks to tap strong investor demand for Nvidia rivals in the AI accelerator market. Alibaba is restructuring T-Head into a standalone business with partial employee ownership, a first step before exploring an eventual listing, though timing and valuation remain uncertain. Alibaba has long invested in chip design to support its cloud and AI ambitions, and positioning T-Head for a future IPO underscores its push to reduce reliance on external suppliers and compete more aggressively in AI hardware.
  • Datadog (DDOG) +3% - Shares rose after Stifel upgraded the stock to Buy from Hold, citing confidence that the company will outperform expectations as core growth accelerates. Analyst Brad Reback said management should be able to guide 2026 revenue to roughly $4.1B, adding that Datadog’s valuation looks appealing given its strengthening fundamentals. Stifel set a $160 price target, implying about 30% upside from the prior close.

Biggest stock losers

  • T1 Energy (TE) -7% - Shares slipped after short seller Culper Research disclosed a bearish position in the stock. The company also filed a prospectus supplement for the potential resale of 14.3M shares and a warrant for 7M additional shares held by existing investors Trina Solar and Stellar Hann Investment. The filing allows those security holders to resell previously issued shares or shares tied to the warrant, though it doesn’t require them to sell or generate any proceeds for the company.
  • Axogen (AXGN) -7% - Shares fell after announcing plans to raise $85M through an underwritten public offering of common stock, with underwriters receiving a 30-day option to buy an additional $12.75M in shares. All shares will be sold by the company, which intends to use the proceeds to repay and terminate its Oberland Capital term loan and fund working capital, capex, and other general corporate needs.

data.title: Alibaba mulls IPO for AI chipmaking unit T-Head: report
data.description:
Alibaba (BABA) is preparing for an IPO of its chipmaking unit T-Head, Bloomberg News reported, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

The company plans to restructure the unit as a business partly owned by employees. The Chinese e-commerce giant will then explore an IPO, though the timing for that remains unclear, the report added.

Alibaba did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

The company is still at the early stages of the process, and it is not clear what the valuation of T-Head could be, the report noted.

Alibaba has long explored chip design, looking to secure a supply of vital components that underpin its data centers and cloud services. AI chips are one part of a broader campaign to become a leading AI company, the report added.

There are indications that T-Head is making progress. Alibaba signed a contract with the country’s number two wireless carrier to deploy its Pingtouge AI accelerators. The chips will go into the mobile operator’s new data center in northwestern China, alongside accelerators provided by competitors MetaX Integrated Circuits and Biren Technology, the report noted.

data.title: Trump considering a ‘tariff-free zone’ for Ukraine - AP
data.description:
U.S. envoy Steve Witkoff said President Donald Trump is considering proposals such as a “tariff-free zone” for Ukraine to help rebuild industry in the war-hit country.

“The president has talked about a tariff-free zone from Ukraine that I think would be game-changing,” Witkoff told the Ukraine breakfast on the sidelines of Davos.

Witkoff made the remarks ahead of a trip to Moscow, as the administration works to broker a peace deal to end the war between Russia and Ukraine. He said he will then travel to Abu Dhabi, where there would be working groups on issues like “military-to-military” and “prosperity” discussions.

The AP contributed to this report.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: First Andes Silver aims to raise $2M via 16.7M share private placement
Skyharbour, Denison Mines launch 2026 Russell Lake uranium exploration
data.description:
Skyharbour Resources (SYHBF) on Thursday unveiled plans for a major 2026 exploration campaign spanning several of the newly formed Russel Lake joint ventures with Denison Mines (DNN).

Over 15,000 meters of diamond drilling is planned across the Wheeler North, RL, and Getty East joint ventures, each of which was formed following the completion of the strategic transaction with Denison in December 2025 that resulted in a reorganization of the former Russell Lake uranium project into four separate property joint ventures.

“2026 is set to be a transformative year for Skyharbour, and the upcoming commencement of drilling at the recently formed Russell Lake joint ventures represents a key near-term catalyst… We are confident that this collaboration with Denison will expedite the discovery process and leverage Denison’s success in exploring, permitting, and developing the neighboring Wheeler River Project…," Jordan Trimble, President and CEO of Skyharbour, said.
